memes.label: "The Doctor's Dilemma"
memes.description: "Title page and act listing"

memes.label: Antitoxin
memes.description: The serum-based treatment central to Ridgeon's medical discovery and the satirical portrayal of medical practice in The Doctor's Dilemma.

memes.label: Art and Aesthetics in The Doctor's Dilemma
memes.description: A cross-entity overview of art and aesthetics in George Bernard Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma, exploring the tension between artistic genius and conventional morality, the artist as outsider, and the play's meditation on beauty, mortality, and creative truth.

memes.label: Art and Beauty
memes.description: The aesthetic values embodied by Louis Dubedat and their challenge to medical materialism in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Art and the Artist in The Doctor's Dilemma
memes.description: Shaw's exploration of the tension between artistic genius and conventional morality in The Doctor's Dilemma — Louis Dubedat embodies the Romantic artist whose aesthetic truth claims exemption from ethical judgment, challenging the medical profession's moral certainties

memes.label: Bacillus and Bacteria
memes.description: The microorganisms at the center of The Doctor's Dilemma's medical debates, from the tubercle bacillus to the diphtheria bacillus, serving as scientific tokens deployed by doctors with varying degrees of genuine understanding.

memes.label: Beauty and Utility
memes.description: The tension between aesthetic value and practical usefulness that structures the moral debate over Louis Dubedat's claim on life and medical resources

memes.label: Blood Poisoning
memes.description: Cutler Walpole's obsessive diagnosis and surgical cure-all, a running satirical motif in The Doctor's Dilemma exposing the reductive monomania of specialist medicine.

memes.label: Sir Ralph Bloomfield Bonington
memes.description: A fashionable and self-satisfied physician in The Doctor's Dilemma — scientifically shallow but socially magnetic, Shaw's satire of medical celebrity

memes.label: Bond Street Gallery
memes.description: A Bond Street picture gallery where Louis Dubedat's posthumous exhibition takes place in Act V — the setting for the play's moral reckoning

memes.label: Cavendish Square
memes.description: Cavendish Square — the medical district in London's West End, referenced in the stage directions of Act I as the neighborhood that defines the professional geography of Ridgeon and the consulting doctors

memes.label: Chloroform and Anaesthesia
memes.description: The chemical agent that made modern surgery possible and a symbol in The Doctor's Dilemma of how technical advance can outstrip professional judgment, referenced through Sir Patrick's dismissal of Walpole as "one of your chloroform surgeons."

memes.label: Conscience
memes.description: The internal moral faculty that both doctors and the artist must navigate in The Doctor's Dilemma — Shaw explores how professional and personal conscience can be suppressed, rationalized, or weaponized.

memes.label: Conventional Morality
memes.description: The unexamined moral assumptions of Edwardian respectable society, which Shaw systematically dismantles through the doctors' judgments of Louis Dubedat

memes.label: Credulity and Superstition in Medicine
memes.description: The irrational faith that sustains the medical marketplace in The Doctor's Dilemma, where Bloomfield Bonington confesses that doctors "spread the superstition" and the public's credulity enables both quackery and genuine medical authority.

memes.label: Cutler Walpole
memes.description: A surgeon whose obsession with the fictional "nuciform sac" operation makes him Shaw's satirical vehicle for critiquing surgical overreach

memes.label: Death and Mortality
memes.description: The representation of death and the medical management of dying in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Disease and Diagnosis
memes.description: The nature of disease and the art of diagnosis as sites of professional contestation in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Dr. Blenkinsop
memes.description: A poor general practitioner in The Doctor's Dilemma whose financial desperation makes him the moral counterweight to Ridgeon's treatment decision

memes.label: Dubedat's Death
memes.description: Louis Dubedat dies in his studio beneath the allegorical figure of Cardinal Death — the direct consequence of Ridgeon's treatment decision, the play's most theatrically ambitious scene, and the moment when medical professionalism's moral corruption is made visible through Shaw's most striking stage image

memes.label: Dubedat's Marriage Proposal
memes.description: Louis Dubedat's marriage proposal to Jennifer — a backstory event that establishes the Dubedats' relationship and whose legal validity is challenged by the revelation of bigamy in Act III of The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Dubedat's Studio
memes.description: Louis Dubedat's artist studio in Chelsea, where the play's central confrontations and his death take place across Acts III and IV

memes.label: Duty and Conscience
memes.description: The tension between professional duty and personal conscience in The Doctor's Dilemma, explored through Ridgeon's moral crisis and Jennifer Dubedat's unwavering loyalty.

memes.label: Emmy
memes.description: Ridgeon's elderly serving-woman in The Doctor's Dilemma — a comic figure who guards the consulting-room door, delivers the news of the knighthood, and manipulates her employer through maternal familiarity

memes.label: General Medical Council
memes.description: The General Medical Council — the statutory regulatory body for the British medical profession, referenced in Shaw's critique of professional self-regulation in The Doctor's Dilemma as an institution that protects doctors rather than patients
aliases: the General Council of Medical Education and Registration

memes.label: Genius and Talent
memes.description: The concept of genius as a moral category and its role in justifying exceptional treatment in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: The Gentleman
memes.description: The contested social ideal of gentlemanly conduct, deployed satirically throughout The Doctor's Dilemma to expose class pretension, professional hypocrisy, and antisemitic prejudice.

memes.label: Germ Theory
memes.description: The scientific understanding that microorganisms cause infectious disease, a foundational concept debated throughout The Doctor's Dilemma as doctors invoke "disease germs" to justify their competing treatments.

memes.label: Harley Street
memes.description: Harley Street — the iconic London medical street adjacent to Cavendish Square, synonymous with elite private medical practice in the Edwardian era and the implicit professional geography of The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Honor and Reputation
memes.description: The role of personal and professional honor in medical decision-making in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Ignorance and Knowledge
memes.description: The tension between medical ignorance and claimed knowledge in The Doctor's Dilemma, where Shaw exposes how professional confidence often substitutes for genuine understanding and how patients' credulity reinforces doctors' pretensions.

memes.label: Inoculation
memes.description: The practice of introducing material from a disease into the body to produce immunity, a recurring point of scientific and historical contention in The Doctor's Dilemma.

memes.label: Jennifer Dubedat
memes.description: Louis Dubedat's wife, whose unwavering devotion to her dishonest husband challenges the doctors' — and the audience's — moral certainties

memes.label: Jennifer's Plea
memes.description: Jennifer Dubedat's emotional appeal to Sir Colenso Ridgeon in Act I, begging him to save her husband Louis — a scene that transforms the clinical consultation into a personal entanglement and seeds Ridgeon's fatal attraction

memes.label: Knighthood
memes.description: The knighthood system in Edwardian England and its role in The Doctor's Dilemma — Ridgeon's recent knighthood frames the play's examination of professional prestige and its corrupting influence on medical judgment.

memes.label: Leo Schutzmacher
memes.description: A Jewish general practitioner in The Doctor's Dilemma whose commercial frankness about medicine exposes the hypocrisy of the more prestigious doctors

memes.label: Louis Dubedat
memes.description: The brilliant but dishonest artist whose tuberculosis treatment — or lack thereof — is the central moral crisis of The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Man and Superman
memes.description: Man and Superman (1903) — George Bernard Shaw's major philosophical comedy, a contemporary work to The Doctor's Dilemma that develops the Life Force theory and the evolving relationship between men and women that informs the later play's treatment of marriage and genius

memes.label: Marriage and Gender
memes.description: Gender dynamics and the institution of marriage as depicted in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Medical Charity
memes.description: The ambiguous role of charity in a market-based medical system as depicted in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Medical Ethics
memes.description: The ethical obligations of doctors and the moral calculus of patient selection in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Medical Experimentation
memes.description: Shaw's critique of doctors who experiment on patients under the guise of treatment, raising profound questions about professional ethics

memes.label: Medical Fashion and Trends
memes.description: Shaw's critique of medical fashion and professional trends in The Doctor's Dilemma, where fashionable treatments and prestigious reputations replace evidence-based judgment and doctors chase the latest therapeutic fad rather than confront the limits of their knowledge.

memes.label: Medical Hierarchy
memes.description: The stratified structure of the Edwardian medical profession, in which rank determines authority, income, and access to patients

memes.label: Medical Profession
memes.description: Shaw's critique of the medical profession in Edwardian England as dramatized in The Doctor's Dilemma (1906)

memes.label: Medical Themes in The Doctor's Dilemma
memes.description: A cross-entity overview of medical themes in George Bernard Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma, covering the science, ethics, economics, and professional culture of Edwardian medicine as dramatized in the play.

memes.label: Minnie Tinwell
memes.description: A hotel maid in The Doctor's Dilemma who reveals herself as Louis Dubedat's legal first wife — a small but pivotal role that exposes Dubedat's moral character

memes.label: Money
memes.description: The economic dimension of medical practice in The Doctor's Dilemma — Shaw examines how money shapes doctor-patient relationships, determines access to care, and corrupts professional judgment.

memes.label: Morality and Ethics
memes.description: The moral and ethical dimensions of The Doctor's Dilemma, where Shaw interrogates conventional morality through the choices of doctors, artists, and their wives, exposing how moral language can justify nearly any action.

memes.label: Nature and Evolution
memes.description: Shaw's philosophy of creative evolution and the concept of nature as a normative framework in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Newspaper Man
memes.description: A cheerful but incompetent journalist in The Doctor's Dilemma who comes to interview the dying Louis Dubedat — a satirical portrait of the press in Edwardian England

memes.label: Nux Vomica and Patent Medicines
memes.description: The commercial medical products that Shaw satirizes in The Doctor's Dilemma through Bloomfield Bonington's enthusiasm for bottled remedies and the play's critique of "scandalous advertisements of patent medicines" as a corrupting force in medical practice.

memes.label: Opsonin
memes.description: Sir Colenso Ridgeon's scientific discovery — a method of measuring the blood's natural immune response to determine the safe timing of inoculation — the central medical idea of The Doctor's Dilemma.

memes.label: Phagocytosis
memes.description: The theory of immune defense by white blood cells, central to Ridgeon's medical discovery and a recurring motif of scientific controversy in The Doctor's Dilemma.

memes.label: Poverty and Class
memes.description: Class inequality and its impact on medical access in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Preface on Doctors
memes.description: Shaw's Preface on Doctors (1906) — the extended polemical essay accompanying The Doctor's Dilemma, arguing for the nationalization of medical services and critiquing the medical profession as a "conspiracy against the laity"

memes.label: Private Practice
memes.description: The economic model of private medical practice in Edwardian London and Shaw's critique of fee-for-service medicine

memes.label: Professional Ethics in Medicine
memes.description: The code of professional conduct governing doctors, challenged and examined through the moral failures of Shaw's medical characters

memes.label: Professional Rivalry
memes.description: Inter-doctor rivalry and professional competition as a driver of the plot in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Quackery
memes.description: Shaw's critique of medical quackery and the commercial exploitation of patient credulity in The Doctor's Dilemma.

memes.label: Redpenny
memes.description: A medical student serving as Ridgeon's secretary in The Doctor's Dilemma — a minor character whose presence establishes the professional hierarchy of the consulting room

memes.label: Religion, Faith, and Medicine
memes.description: The relationship between religion, faith, and medical practice in The Doctor's Dilemma, where Shaw explores how belief systems — both religious and secular — shape patients' expectations, doctors' authority, and the meaning of illness and healing.

memes.label: Reputation and Scandal
memes.description: The social mechanisms by which professional reputation is constructed and destroyed, explored through the public exposure of Louis Dubedat's misconduct

memes.label: Ridgeon's Breakthrough
memes.description: Ridgeon's discovery of the opsonin index — the medical breakthrough that earns him a knighthood and provides the treatment around which the central moral dilemma of The Doctor's Dilemma turns

memes.label: Ridgeon's Consulting Room
memes.description: Sir Colenso Ridgeon's consulting room at 409 Queen Anne Street, Marylebone — the primary setting of Act I of The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Ridgeon's Dilemma
memes.description: The central moral crisis of Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma — when Ridgeon must choose which tuberculosis patient lives and which dies, the utilitarian calculus of his choice exposes the corruption of a medical profession that judges worth before it heals illness

memes.label: Ridgeon's Home
memes.description: Ridgeon's home on Queen Anne Street — the private residence and consulting room that together define Ridgeon's professional and domestic sphere, where the medical and the personal intersect in Act I of The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Ridgeon's Laboratory
memes.description: Ridgeon's research laboratory — the unseen but crucial setting where the opsonin treatment is developed and the play's scientific claims originate, representing the promise and limitation of laboratory medicine in Shaw's critique of the medical profession

memes.label: Royal College of Physicians
memes.description: The Royal College of Physicians — the professional body representing elite physicians in Edwardian London, implied as the institutional framework behind the medical establishment that Shaw satirizes in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: The theme of sacrifice in The Doctor's Dilemma — personal, artistic, and medical sacrifice as competing moral frameworks.

memes.label: Sanitation and Public Health
memes.description: The field of preventive medicine and environmental health improvement referenced in The Doctor's Dilemma, representing the alternative to individual curative treatment that Shaw championed as the true solution to diseases of poverty like tuberculosis.

memes.label: Dr. Leo Schutzmacher
memes.description: A general practitioner in The Doctor's Dilemma who made his fortune through the "Cure Guaranteed" — a placebo-style practice that Shaw uses to satirize the economics of private medicine.

memes.label: Scientific Medicine
memes.description: The rise of laboratory-based scientific medicine in Edwardian England as satirized by Shaw in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Scientific Method and Experiment
memes.description: The role of scientific method and experimental research in The Doctor's Dilemma, centered on Ridgeon's laboratory discoveries and Shaw's examination of how genuine science coexists uneasily with clinical practice and professional ambition.

memes.label: Scoundrel
memes.description: The moral category that Louis Dubedat embodies in The Doctor's Dilemma — Shaw complicates the figure of the scoundrel by making him also a genius, forcing the audience to confront the relationship between creative power and moral character.

memes.label: Scoundrel as Artist
memes.description: The central paradox of The Doctor's Dilemma — whether a morally bankrupt man can also be a great artist — embodied in the character of Louis Dubedat

memes.label: Selfishness and Altruism
memes.description: The dialectic of selfishness and altruism in The Doctor's Dilemma, where Shaw shows that apparent selflessness often masks self-interest and that genuine generosity is rarer and more complex than moral convention acknowledges.

memes.label: Sex and Gender in Victorian Society
memes.description: The gendered power structures of Edwardian society as reflected in marriage, medicine, and moral judgment in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: Sir Colenso Ridgeon
memes.description: The protagonist of Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma — a newly knighted physician whose opsonin treatment for tuberculosis and the moral choices it forces drive the play's central conflict between medical professionalism and ethical responsibility

memes.label: Sir Patrick Cullen
memes.description: The elder statesman of the medical profession in Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma — a plain-spoken Irish physician whose aphoristic wisdom serves as the play's moral compass and whose famous pronouncement "All professions are conspiracies against the laity" defines the play's central thesis

memes.label: Sir Ralph Bloomfield Bonington
memes.description: A fashionable and confident physician in The Doctor's Dilemma whose enthusiastic misunderstanding of antitoxin therapy provides much of the play's medical satire.

memes.label: Social Criticism in The Doctor's Dilemma
memes.description: A cross-entity overview of social criticism and satire in George Bernard Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma, covering class inequality, professional hypocrisy, gender dynamics, and the failures of Edwardian institutions.

memes.label: St. Anne's Hospital
memes.description: St. Anne's Hospital — Ridgeon's institutional affiliation, referenced as the site of his opsonin research and the institutional context for the medical world depicted in Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: The Star and Garter Consultation
memes.description: The doctors' after-dinner discussion of Louis Dubedat's case on the Star and Garter Hotel terrace — a medical consultation conducted as a social event that exposes the entanglement of professional judgment with class and leisure

memes.label: Star and Garter Hotel
memes.description: The Star and Garter Hotel terrace at Richmond, where the doctors dine with the Dubedats in Act II — a fashionable Edwardian dining destination whose social setting displaces medical judgment from the clinic to the dinner table, exposing how professional decisions are entangled with class and leisure

memes.label: Statistics and Evidence in Medicine
memes.description: The quantified approach to medical decision-making embodied in Ridgeon's opsonin index — "If the figure is one, inoculate and cure" — and the skeptical counterpoint offered by Sir Patrick's demand for real-world evidence over laboratory theory.

memes.label: Surgery in The Doctor's Dilemma
memes.description: The practice of surgery as portrayed in The Doctor's Dilemma through Cutler Walpole's obsessive surgical monomania, revealing Shaw's critique of invasive treatment as a commercial enterprise in Edwardian medicine.

memes.label: The Artist as Social Critic
memes.description: The concept of the artist who exposes society's hypocrisies through creative work, embodied in Louis Dubedat's role as truth-teller and deceiver

memes.label: The Cure Motif
memes.description: The recurring motif of "cure" in The Doctor's Dilemma, from Schutzmacher's commercial slogans to Ridgeon's opsonin treatment, as a lens through which Shaw critiques medical pretension and the gap between therapeutic promise and scientific reality.

memes.label: The Doctor as Businessman
memes.description: Shaw's examination of the doctor as commercial operator in The Doctor's Dilemma, where medical practice is shown to be a business transaction and the professional boundary between healing and selling becomes dangerously blurred.

memes.label: The Doctor-Patient Relationship
memes.description: The power dynamics and ethical obligations of the doctor-patient relationship as critiqued in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: The Doctors' Decision
memes.description: The collective decision by Ridgeon and his colleagues to refuse treatment to Louis Dubedat and instead treat Dr. Blenkinsop — the central moral action of The Doctor's Dilemma, made as an after-dinner social ritual on the Star and Garter terrace

memes.label: The Doctor's Dilemma (Play)
memes.description: George Bernard Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma (1906) — a five-act satirical comedy about the medical profession in Edwardian England, in which a newly knighted physician must choose which tuberculosis patient to save, exposing the corruption beneath medical professionalism

memes.label: The Initial Diagnosis
memes.description: Jennifer Dubedat's initial visit to Sir Colenso Ridgeon in Act I, where she pleads for him to treat her husband Louis — a consultation that sets the entire moral drama of The Doctor's Dilemma in motion

memes.label: The Knighthood Reception
memes.description: The morning of Ridgeon's knighthood, when his medical colleagues call to congratulate him — a parade of professional types that establishes the play's satirical anatomy of Edwardian medicine

memes.label: The Medical Profession (as Institution)
memes.description: The medical profession as an institution in Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma — a self-regulating monopoly whose claim to scientific authority masks its economic self-interest, portrayed through six representative doctors and critiqued in both the play and its polemical Preface

memes.label: The Medical Profession in The Doctor's Dilemma
memes.description: Shaw's satirical portrayal of the Edwardian medical profession in The Doctor's Dilemma — a "conspiracy against the laity" in which professional authority masks economic self-interest and medical judgment conceals class prejudice

memes.label: The Nature of Healing
memes.description: Shaw's examination of what healing actually is in The Doctor's Dilemma — whether it comes from medical intervention, the body's natural processes, or the doctor's reassuring presence — and how the profession systematically obscures this question.

memes.label: The Opening Reception
memes.description: The opening reception of Louis Dubedat's posthumous exhibition at the Bond Street gallery in Act V — a social gathering where the late artist is celebrated and Ridgeon's final confession shatters the occasion

memes.label: The Philanderer
memes.description: The Philanderer (1893) — George Bernard Shaw's early play from the "Plays Unpleasant" collection, a comedy exploring marriage, the "New Woman," and Ibsenite themes that anticipate Shaw's later treatment of medical and social institutions in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: The Placebo Effect
memes.description: The medical phenomenon in which patient belief drives therapeutic outcomes, satirized through Bloomfield Bonington's indiscriminate use of antitoxin

memes.label: The Posthumous Exhibition
memes.description: Louis Dubedat's posthumous exhibition at a Bond Street gallery — the artist's vindication and the doctors' moral reckoning in Act V

memes.label: The Registry Office
memes.description: The Registry Office — the civil marriage registration office referenced in Act III discussions of Louis and Jennifer Dubedat's marriage, whose legal status is called into question through implications of bigamy in The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: The Royal Academy
memes.description: The Royal Academy of Arts — London's premier art institution, referenced in The Doctor's Dilemma in connection with Louis Dubedat's artistic ambitions and the institutional validation he craves but never achieves in his lifetime

memes.label: The Secretary
memes.description: The secretary at the Bond Street Picture Gallery in The Doctor's Dilemma — a fashionably dressed functionary who manages the practical details of Dubedat's posthumous exhibition

memes.label: The Studio Confrontation
memes.description: The doctors confront Louis Dubedat in his studio about his dishonesty — a moral tribunal that reverses the power dynamics of the consulting room and exposes the limits of professional authority

memes.label: The Truth Revealed
memes.description: The final revelation in Act V when Ridgeon confesses to Jennifer that he deliberately let Louis Dubedat die — the climactic moment of truth that shatters the moral and emotional equilibrium of The Doctor's Dilemma

memes.label: The Waiter
memes.description: A waiter at the Star and Garter Hotel in The Doctor's Dilemma — a single-line character whose brief appearance punctuates the end of the Act II dinner scene

relations.label: dinner at the Star and Garter

memes.label: Truth and Deception
memes.description: The moral status of truth-telling and deception in The Doctor's Dilemma, centered on Louis Dubedat's fabrications

memes.label: Tuberculosis
memes.description: The disease afflicting Louis Dubedat in The Doctor's Dilemma — tuberculosis serves as both a medical reality and a dramatic device that frames the play's central moral dilemma.

memes.label: Tuberculosis and Consumption
memes.description: The disease that drives the central plot of The Doctor's Dilemma — Louis Dubedat's tuberculosis forces Ridgeon to choose which patient lives — while serving as a metaphor for Shaw's critique of medical practice in Edwardian England.

memes.label: Vaccination
memes.description: The practice of preventive inoculation debated in The Doctor's Dilemma, where Ridgeon declares his opsonin treatment "ten times more important than vaccination" and Shaw explores the limits of vaccine science in Edwardian medicine.

memes.label: Vivisection
memes.description: The practice of animal experimentation referenced in The Doctor's Dilemma's Preface on Doctors, where Shaw critiques the medical profession's willingness to experiment on living subjects in the pursuit of knowledge.

memes.label: Wealth and Social Status
memes.description: The role of wealth and social status in determining access to medical care and professional standing in Shaw's The Doctor's Dilemma, examined through the contrasting fortunes of Ridgeon, Sir Patrick, Schutzmacher, and Louis Dubedat.

memes.label: Widowers' Houses
memes.description: Widowers' Houses (1892) — George Bernard Shaw's first play, dealing with slum landlordism and social critique, establishing the polemical method that would reach full expression in The Doctor's Dilemma
